paper_title: Foraminiferal analysis and paleoenvironments of sediments in well -Aa, deepwater offshore, Niger Delta, Nigeria

methods.description: Process for preparing ditch cutting samples for lithologic, texture, and fossil content analysis using a Stereo-binocular microscope and gamma ray log data.

steps.description: Twenty-five (25) ditch cutting samples from well AA, Offshore Niger Delta, at depth interval of 9000ft-11025ft and Gamma ray log data were provided by Shell Petroleum Company, Nigeria.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain ditch cutting samples and gamma ray log data

steps.description: The ditch cutting samples were subjected to depth by depth analysis under a Stereo-binocular microscope to determine the lithologic, texture and fossil content following the procedures in by Swanson, (1981).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze samples under microscope for lithologic, texture, and fossil content
outputs: lithologic, texture, and fossil content data

steps.description: Gamma ray log measures the differences in radioactivity between shales and sands, with a scale ranging from 0 -150 API units. The higher and lower gamma ray readings were used to indicate the shale and sand units respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: interpret gamma ray log data to differentiate shale and sand units

steps.description: About 25g of each ditch cutting samples was placed into aluminium sample preparation pan that were prelabelled. Each sample was soaked in Hydrogen peroxide overnight to disaggregate, and later decanted, topped with water and left overnight.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare samples by soaking in hydrogen peroxide and decanting

steps.description: Each samples was washed with distil water through the 63 micron sieve mesh and dried in an oven at temperature of 70ᵒC.
steps.searchable_summary: wash samples through sieve and dry in oven

steps.description: The dried samples were later left to cool and subseqently put in labelled sachets bags for further examinations.
steps.searchable_summary: cool and package samples for examination

steps.description: The packaged samples were taken under the microscope for identification of foraminifera species, with the guidiance of appropriate Foraminifera catalogues and literatures, including Loeblich and Tappan (1964,1988); Cushman (1969).
steps.searchable_summary: identify foraminifera species using microscope and literature

steps.description: A check list was made of all foraminifera recovered and a distribution chart was produced.
steps.searchable_summary: create checklist and distribution chart of foraminifera

steps.description: Photomonographs of some of the species recovered were taken (Figure 3).
